deep_dive.title: Jailbreak to Protect: Buffering and Reinforcing via Temporary Jailbreaking for Safe Fine-Tuning in Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Fine-tuning-as-a-Service (FaaS) enables personalization of large language models (LLMs), but it can weaken safety-alignment under harmful fine-tuning attacks. Recent work has shown that activating harmful-behavior modules during fine-tuning can prevent models from learning undesired behaviors, but its mechanism remains unclear. In this paper, we revisit temporary jailbreaking as a defense against harmful fine-tuning and provide a gradient-level analysis showing that it saturates safety-degrading gradients while preserving benign task-relevant gradients. Based on this insight, we propose a Buffer-and-Reinforce fine-tuning framework that buffers harmful updates during user fine-tuning and reinforces safety after adaptation. Specifically, BufferLoRA induces temporary jailbreaking as a removable adapter to reduce harmful updates during user fine-tuning. After adaptation, ReinforceLoRA, trained to recover refusal behavior under the temporarily jailbroken state, is integrated with UserLoRA via QR decomposition-based merging to reinforce safety while preserving user-task performance. Extensive experiments show that our framework achieves superior safety and utility with no additional safety data during user fine-tuning and minimal computational cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: defense against harmful fine-tuning attacks via temporary jailbreaking
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过临时越狱防御有害微调攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过临时越狱防御有害微调攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过临时越狱防御有害微调攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Buffer-and-Reinforce framework that temporarily jailbreaks the model during fine-tuning to saturate safety-degrading gradients while preserving benign task gradients, directly defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对有害微调攻击导致的安全对齐弱化问题，提出了一种缓冲-强化微调框架。该框架在微调过程中临时激活有害行为模块（即临时越狱），通过梯度级分析发现这能饱和安全退化梯度同时保留良性任务梯度。实验表明该方法有效防御有害微调攻击，为安全微调提供了一种无需混合安全数据的新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对有害微调攻击导致的安全对齐弱化问题，提出了一种缓冲-强化微调框架。该框架在微调过程中临时激活有害行为模块（即临时越狱），通过梯度级分析发现这能饱和安全退化梯度同时保留良性任务梯度。实验表明该方法有效防御有害微调攻击，为安全微调提供了一种无需混合安全数据的新思路。

deep_dive.title: From Parameters to Data: A Task-Parameter-Guided Fine-Tuning Pipeline for Efficient LLM Alignment
deep_dive.abstract: Adapting Large Language Models (LLMs) to specialized domains typically incurs high data and computational overhead. While prior efficiency efforts have largely treated data selection and parameter-efficient fine-tuning as isolated processes, our empirical analysis suggests they may be intrinsically coupled. We posit the Strong Map Hypothesis: a sparse subset of attention heads plays a dominant role in task-specific adaptation, acting as keys that unlock specific data patterns. Building on this observation, we propose From Parameters to Data (P2D), a unified framework that leverages these task-sensitive attention heads as a dual compass for both sample mining and structural pruning. To rigorously quantify the total pipeline cost, we introduce the Alignment Efficiency Ratio (AER) metric for both selection latency and training time. Mechanistically, P2D identifies critical heads via a lightweight proxy and uses them as a functional filter to curate high-affinity data, establishing a synergistic pipeline. Empirically, by updating merely 10% of attention heads on 10% of the data, P2D achieves an 8.3 pp performance gain over strong baselines and delivers a 7.0x end-to-end time speedup. These results validate that precise parameter-data synchronization eliminates redundancy, offering a new paradigm for efficient alignment.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: task-parameter-guided data selection for efficient LLM alignment fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 任务参数引导的数据选择用于高效LLM对齐微调
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 任务参数引导的数据选择用于高效LLM对齐微调
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 任务参数引导的数据选择用于高效LLM对齐微调
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes P2D framework that uses task-sensitive attention heads to guide data selection and pruning for efficient LLM alignment fine-tuning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM领域微调中数据选择与参数高效微调孤立进行的问题，提出从参数到数据（P2D）统一框架。基于强映射假设，发现稀疏注意力头主导任务适应，利用这些头部作为指南针同时进行样本挖掘和结构化剪枝。实验证明该方法能有效筛选高质量训练数据并降低计算开销，为微调中的数据策展提供了新思路，可直接应用于安全对齐数据的选择。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对LLM领域微调中数据选择与参数高效微调孤立进行的问题，提出从参数到数据（P2D）统一框架。基于强映射假设，发现稀疏注意力头主导任务适应，利用这些头部作为指南针同时进行样本挖掘和结构化剪枝。实验证明该方法能有效筛选高质量训练数据并降低计算开销，为微调中的数据策展提供了新思路，可直接应用于安全对齐数据的选择。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning

deep_dive.title: On-Policy Consistency Training Improves LLM Safety with Minimal Capability Degradation
deep_dive.abstract: Aligned models can misbehave in several ways: they are often sycophantic, fall victim to jailbreaks, or fail to include appropriate safety warnings. Consistency training is a promising new alignment paradigm to mitigate such failures by training invariants into the model using contrastive input pairs. Existing consistency training procedures generate the supervision signal once, offline, and use supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to update the model. Unfortunately, the resulting models tend to merely memorize the surface forms of the training distribution and thus generalize poorly and regress in their capabilities. We introduce On-Policy Consistency Training (OPCT), a new consistency training approach where the objective is computed over the model's own responses to prompts, supervised by itself conditioned on corresponding contrastive prompts. We evaluate OPCT on three safety axes: sycophancy, jailbreaking, and safety awareness. Across three model families, OPCT outperforms its SFT counterpart on all safety desiderata. It nearly halves the sycophancy rate relative to baseline (8.1% vs. 15.4%, compared to 11.2% for SFT). Under an adaptive per-target attacker, OPCT holds jailbreak defense success near 99% on held-out jailbreak behaviors, whereas SFT achieves 87% on average. On safety awareness, OPCT outperforms SFT in two out of three models, and matches it on the other. OPCT also largely avoids the capability regressions that SFT induces, such as a 28-point drop on MATH-500. Our results suggest that consistency training is best implemented as OPCT rather than as SFT, especially when generalization beyond the training distribution is desired.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: improves LLM safety against jailbreaks via consistency training on contrastive pairs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过对比配对的一致性训练提升LLM对抗越狱攻击的安全性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过对比配对的一致性训练提升LLM对抗越狱攻击的安全性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过对比配对的一致性训练提升LLM对抗越狱攻击的安全性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes On-Policy Consistency Training (OPCT) to improve LLM safety by training invariants using contrastive input pairs, reducing jailbreak success with minimal capability loss.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有一致性训练因离线生成监督信号导致模型泛化差、能力退化的问题，提出在线策略一致性训练（OPCT）方法。该方法在模型当前策略下计算一致性目标，通过对比输入对训练不变性，有效提升模型对越狱攻击的抵抗能力，同时保持模型能力几乎不退化。实验表明OPCT显著降低越狱成功率，是一种高效的安全对齐方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有一致性训练因离线生成监督信号导致模型泛化差、能力退化的问题，提出在线策略一致性训练（OPCT）方法。该方法在模型当前策略下计算一致性目标，通过对比输入对训练不变性，有效提升模型对越狱攻击的抵抗能力，同时保持模型能力几乎不退化。实验表明OPCT显著降低越狱成功率，是一种高效的安全对齐方法。

deep_dive.title: Steering Beyond the Support: Adversarial Training on Unsupervised Jailbroken Activation Simulation
deep_dive.abstract: Jailbreak prompts can trigger harmful completions on aligned LLMs, In accordance, safety steering has been proposed: test-time activation interventions that steer jailbreak activations to trigger refusal while preserving benign utility. However, existing steering methods are fundamentally supervised and tied to a static, limited training set, whereas real jailbreaks evolve and are often out-of-distributed from the training set, leading to failures on unseen attacks. In this paper, we tackle the failure on unseen jailbreaks problem, base on unsupervised latent direction discovery. We propose a bi-level adversarial training framework for zero-shot jailbreak defense. In the inner step, we simulate diverse jail-broken activations by extrapolating from refusal-state harmful-request activations via unsupervised latent direction discovery, which expands the coverage of real jailbreak activation subspaces. In the outer step, we train a potential-induced steering field to push these adversarial jailbroken states into refusal regions while keeping benign unchanged. Across three LLMs and six classical jailbreak families, our method achieves strong defense with attack success rates mostly below 5%, and rising subspace coverage throughout training helps explain the improved generalization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: activation steering for jailbreak defense via unsupervised activation simulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过无监督激活模拟进行激活引导以防御越狱
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过无监督激活模拟进行激活引导以防御越狱
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过无监督激活模拟进行激活引导以防御越狱
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a bi-level adversarial training framework that simulates diverse jailbroken activations to learn robust steering directions for zero-shot jailbreak defense.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有激活引导方法在不可见越狱攻击下失效的问题，提出了一种基于无监督潜在方向发现的两层对抗训练框架。在内层，通过模拟多样化的越狱激活状态来生成训练数据；在外层，利用这些模拟数据学习鲁棒的引导方向。实验表明该方法在零样本越狱防御中显著优于现有方法，为安全对齐数据的生成和防御策略提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对现有激活引导方法在不可见越狱攻击下失效的问题，提出了一种基于无监督潜在方向发现的两层对抗训练框架。在内层，通过模拟多样化的越狱激活状态来生成训练数据；在外层，利用这些模拟数据学习鲁棒的引导方向。实验表明该方法在零样本越狱防御中显著优于现有方法，为安全对齐数据的生成和防御策略提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: PoisonForge: Task-Level Targeted Poisoning Benchmark for Instruction-Tuned LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: When practitioners fine-tune LLMs on unvetted datasets, an adversary can exploit the data supply chain through task-level poisoning: inserting a small number of crafted instruction-response pairs that cause the model to embed attacker-specified entities, such as a country, in outputs for a targeted task family while behaving normally elsewhere. We introduce PoisonForge, a benchmark that parameterizes this threat along four dimensions (bias type, poisoning mode, appearance count, and target output length) and evaluates 12 open-weight models (from 2B to 32B parameters) across five families under a primarily 1% poison budget. With only 10 poisoned examples among 1,000 fine-tuning examples, 11 of 12 models exceed a 70% attack success rate (ASR) in their most vulnerable configuration. Meanwhile, unintended leakage to non-target tasks remains below 0.5%, and models perform well on standard benchmarks. We analyze in detail the factors contributing to attack success. We observe that multiple appearances of an entity increase the ASR, the optimal poisoning mode depends on the semantic structure of the target entity, and ASR drops monotonically with the task output length. A correlation analysis and risk prediction model confirm that poisoning design choices, rather than model scale, are the primary causes of attack success, and that these patterns generalize to predict attack success on new tasks. We release all configurations, pipelines, and analysis code to support reproducible comparisons.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for harmful fine-tuning attacks, informing defense strategies
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对有害微调攻击的基准测试，为防御策略提供参考
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对有害微调攻击的基准测试，为防御策略提供参考
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对有害微调攻击的基准测试，为防御策略提供参考
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces PoisonForge, a benchmark for task-level poisoning during instruction-tuning, revealing high attack success rates with as few as 10 poisoned examples.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出PoisonForge基准，系统研究了指令微调过程中的任务级投毒攻击。通过参数化攻击的四维属性，并在12个开源模型上评估，发现仅需10个投毒样例（1%预算）即可在11个模型上实现超过70%的攻击成功率。该工作为理解有害微调攻击的威胁提供了重要工具，为后续防御措施（如安全数据混合）的设计和评估奠定了基础。

quick_skim.title: Detecting Trojaned DNNs via Spectral Regression Analysis
quick_skim.abstract: Modern DNNs are repeatedly fine-tuned to incorporate new data and functionality. This evolutionary workflow introduces a security risk when updated data cannot be fully trusted, as adversaries may implant Trojans during fine-tuning. We present MIST, a Trojan detection approach that analyzes how a model's internal representations change during fine-tuning. Rather than attempting to reconstruct trigger conditions, MIST characterizes benign model evolution using pre-activation spectra and flags updates whose spectral deviations are inconsistent with this reference. This framing treats Trojan detection as a regression problem over model updates. An empirical evaluation across four datasets and eight Trojan attacks shows that spectral distances reliably distinguish Trojaned updates from clean fine-tuning. MIST outperforms state-of-the-art detection accuracy after a single update, without requiring any knowledge about the poisoned data or the trigger, and remains effective under multi-step benign evolution, with graceful and bounded degradation. These results indicate that spectral evolution provides a stable and assumption-light signal for detecting malicious model updates.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: detects Trojan attacks during fine-tuning using spectral analysis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用光谱分析检测微调过程中的木马攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用光谱分析检测微调过程中的木马攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用光谱分析检测微调过程中的木马攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MIST, a Trojan detection method that analyzes spectral changes in representations during fine-tuning to detect attacks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 深度神经网络在微调过程中可能被植入木马，现有方法需重构触发器。本文提出MIST，通过分析微调前后表示的光谱变化来检测异常，将木马检测转化为回归问题。在多个数据集和攻击方法上验证了有效性，为防御有害微调攻击提供了轻量级检测手段。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 深度神经网络在微调过程中可能被植入木马，现有方法需重构触发器。本文提出MIST，通过分析微调前后表示的光谱变化来检测异常，将木马检测转化为回归问题。在多个数据集和攻击方法上验证了有效性，为防御有害微调攻击提供了轻量级检测手段。

quick_skim.title: Towards Context-Invariant Safety Alignment for Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Preference-based post-training aligns LLMs with human intent, yet safety behavior often remains brittle. A model may refuse a harmful request in a standard prompt but comply when the same intent is wrapped in adversarial wording. We suggest that robust safety requires context-invariant alignment, where behavior depends on the underlying intent rather than surface form. Enforcing invariance is difficult in alignment because not all training signals are equally trustworthy; for some prompt variants we can obtain verifiable feedback (e.g., multiple-choice), while for open-ended variants we typically rely on noisy, gameable reward proxies (e.g., learned judges). As a result, standard symmetric invariance regularizers can reduce cross-context discrepancies by lowering performance on reliable variants instead of improving open-ended robustness. To address this, we introduce Anchor Invariance Regularization (AIR), which treats verifiable prompts as anchors and uses a stop-gradient target to regularize only the open-ended variants toward the anchor performance. AIR is implemented as a plug-in auxiliary loss and combined with group-based preference optimization (e.g., GRPO) via heterogeneous prompt grouping. Across Safety, Moral Reasoning, and Math, AIR improves context invariance, boosting in-distribution group accuracy by 12.71% and out-of-distribution consistency by 33.49%, making safety constraints robust to adversarial framings.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes context-invariant safety alignment to improve robustness against adversarial wording
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出上下文不变安全对齐以提高对抗措辞的鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出上下文不变安全对齐以提高对抗措辞的鲁棒性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出上下文不变安全对齐以提高对抗措辞的鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper argues that robust safety requires context-invariant alignment, where behavior depends on intent not surface form, and proposes methods to enforce invariance under noisy reward proxies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文指出当前LLM安全对齐在面对对抗性措辞时存在脆弱性，提出安全行为应依赖于内在意图而非表面形式的上下文不变对齐原则。由于训练信号可信度不同，标准对称正则化难以直接应用。作者提出一种利用可验证反馈和噪声奖励代理结合的方法来强化对齐不变性。该方法有助于提升模型在微调后的安全鲁棒性，对对齐保持研究具有参考价值。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

quick_skim.title: Causal Physics Steering in Video World Models via Concept Activation Vectors
quick_skim.abstract: Video world models learn representations of physical dynamics, but controlling their physical expectations at inference time remains an open problem. Recent interpretability work identified a Physics Emergence Zone (PEZ), a group of middle transformer layers in VideoMAE where physical plausibility is represented separately from other visual features. However, it remained unclear whether this structure could be used to directly control the model's physics reasoning. We present physics steering, a training-free method that uses the weight vector of a linear probe at a PEZ layer as a Concept Activation Vector (CAV) and injects it into hidden states during inference. This shifts the model's physical expectations without changing any model weights. On the IntPhys benchmark, this intervention reliably shifts the model's plausibility judgment in either direction, depending on the steering sign. The effect appears only when the intervention is applied within the Physics Emergence Zone, suggesting that the relevant physics representation is localized there. We further find that physics is encoded separately from motion direction, and that different intuitive physics principles occupy distinct directions within this representation space. Together, these results show that physical reasoning in VideoMAE is not only readable, but also directly steerable.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses Concept Activation Vectors for steering model behavior
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用概念激活向量引导模型行为
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用概念激活向量引导模型行为
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用概念激活向量引导模型行为
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A training-free method to steer physical expectations in video world models by injecting Concept Activation Vectors from linear probes into hidden states, enabling control over physics reasoning without weight changes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对视频世界模型中物理动力学推理的控制问题，提出了一种无需训练的物理引导方法。通过将从线性探针提取的概念激活向量注入到中间隐藏状态，在不改变模型权重的前提下调整模型的物理预期。实验表明该方法能够有效控制模型对物理合理性的判断，为模型行为干预提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: SafeMed-R1: Clinician-Audited Safety and Ethics Alignment for Medical Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models(LLMs) increasingly match expert performance on licensing examinations, yet routine clinical use remains limited because governance requires auditable reasoning, safety and ethics alignment, and resilience to adversarial misuse. Here we present SafeMed-R1, trained with a traceable Clinical Trust Signals(CTS) pipeline that links each reasoning instance to clinician rubric scores and edit histories, and aligned through safety and ethics supervision and red team stress testing. SafeMed-R1 attains a macro-averaged accuracy of 79.6% across clinical benchmarks. Under adversarial safety testing, it shows the lowest aggregated risk and reduces unsafe outputs by about 3 to 5% relative to its baseline. In a paired expert study of 30 medication safety vignettes, SafeMed-R1 matches PGY1 and PGY2 residents on medical correctness and scores higher for medication safety, guideline consistency, and clinical usefulness. Collectively, these results suggest that clinician-audited supervision provenance, together with domain-tailored safety and ethics alignment, can strengthen governance-relevant evidence without relying on inference-time retrieval or citation grounding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: clinician-audited safety data curation and alignment for medical LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 临床医生审核的安全数据策展和医疗LLM对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 临床医生审核的安全数据策展和医疗LLM对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 临床医生审核的安全数据策展和医疗LLM对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents SafeMed-R1, a medical LLM aligned with clinician-audited safety data pipeline, showing reduced unsafe outputs and low adversarial risk.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对医疗LLM缺乏可审计安全对齐的问题，提出SafeMed-R1，构建基于临床医生评分的可信信号流程来策展安全数据并进行对齐。通过红队压力测试和安全性监督训练，模型在临床基准上达到79.6%准确率，并将不安全输出降低3-5%，在对抗测试中风险最低。该方法为医疗领域的安全对齐提供了可审计的解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对医疗LLM缺乏可审计安全对齐的问题，提出SafeMed-R1，构建基于临床医生评分的可信信号流程来策展安全数据并进行对齐。通过红队压力测试和安全性监督训练，模型在临床基准上达到79.6%准确率，并将不安全输出降低3-5%，在对抗测试中风险最低。该方法为医疗领域的安全对齐提供了可审计的解决方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: curating safety data for alignment preservation
